pi_items.source: Multiple authoritative medical sources (PubMed, MedlinePlus, FDA, DrugBank, Mayo Clinic) confirm all four drugs are real: Sunitinib and Pazopanib are both FDA-approved tyrosine kinase inhibitors for renal cell carcinoma treatment; Warfarin (Coumadin) is a vitamin K antagonist anticoagulant; Apixaban (Eliquis) is a Factor Xa inhibitor anticoagulant.

pi_items.verified_value: FDA-approved for metastatic renal cell carcinoma (mRCC) and adjuvant treatment of high-risk RCC following nephrectomy
pi_items.source: FDA drug approval database and NEJM publications confirm Sunitinib (Sutent, Pfizer) received FDA approval in January 2006 for mRCC and on November 16, 2017 for adjuvant RCC treatment

pi_items.verified_value: FDA-approved for metastatic renal cell carcinoma as first-line and post-cytokine treatment
pi_items.source: FDA approval and clinical trial data (NEJM, Journal of Clinical Oncology) confirm Pazopanib (Votrient) was approved by US FDA in October 2009 and EMA in June 2010 for metastatic RCC treatment

MedlinePlus, Cleveland Clinic, FDA, DrugBank - all confirm Warfarin (Coumadin) is an anticoagulant used to prevent blood clots in atrial fibrillation, venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, and after myocardial infarction

pi_items.verified_value: Factor Xa inhibitor anticoagulant (DOAC - Direct Oral Anticoagulant)
pi_items.source: MedlinePlus, Mayo Clinic, FDA, DrugBank - all confirm Apixaban (Eliquis) is an anticoagulant used to prevent blood clots in atrial fibrillation, DVT/PE treatment and prevention, and post-surgical prophylaxis
Confirmed in task.json description, setup_task.sh (line 10 removes this file), export_result.sh (line 7 defines OUTPUT_FILE), and verifier.py (line 59 checks for file existence)

Official Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions documentation and cancer-druginteractions.org website state the Traffic Light system uses exactly four colors: RED (contraindication/should not coadminister), AMBER (potential clinically significant interaction requiring monitoring), YELLOW (weak interaction unlikely to require monitoring), GREEN (no interaction). The metadata includes "orange" (not used) and "grey"/"gray" (not part of the official system). The task description also incorrectly lists "Orange" and "Grey" instead of "Amber".

pi_items.verified_value: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive (com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology) - real Android app containing the Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database
pi_items.source: APK file verified as legitimate zip archive at /sdcard/scripts/apks/com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology.apk; app is available on Google Play and Apple App Store; developed by Radboud UMC (Nijmegen) and University of Liverpool Drug Interactions Group; database last updated June 2022 before website took offline in September 2023

pi_items.verified_value: Documented increased risk of bleeding - warfarin requires close INR monitoring when used with sunitinib due to potential CYP3A4 pathway interaction
pi_items.source: cancer-druginteractions.org and clinical literature document that sunitinib + warfarin (coumarins) shows increased bleeding risk and requires INR/PT monitoring

pi_items.verified_value: Unlikely clinically significant interaction - apixaban metabolized by CYP3A4 but sunitinib does not inhibit/induce CYP450 enzymes
pi_items.source: cancer-druginteractions.org states: coadministration has not been studied but based on metabolism and clearance a clinically significant interaction is unlikely

pi_items.verified_value: Documented interaction with increased PT-INR risk - clinical trials showed minimal pharmacokinetic effect but case reports show clinically significant interactions requiring INR monitoring
pi_items.source: PubMed case reports and clinical pharmacology studies document pazopanib-warfarin interaction with PT-INR prolongation; close monitoring of PT-INR is warranted when coadministered

pi_items.verified_value: Apixaban increases pazopanib exposure through P-glycoprotein (MDR1) efflux transporter - interaction requires monitoring
pi_items.source: Clinical literature indicates apixaban will increase the level or effect of pazopanib through transporter effects, requiring caution and monitoring

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app (com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology), which contains the genuine Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database. All four drugs are real: Sunitinib and Pazopanib are FDA-approved tyrosine kinase inhibitors for renal cell carcinoma; Warfarin and Apixaban are real anticoagulants. The database uses a Traffic Light interaction system with RED (contraindication), AMBER (significant interaction requiring monitoring), YELLOW (weak interaction), and GREEN (no interaction). CRITICAL FINDING: The metadata and task description incorrectly list "orange" and "grey"/"gray" as valid colors; the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart system uses AMBER (not ORANGE) and does NOT use GREY. All documented drug interactions (Sunitinib-Warfarin increased bleeding risk, Pazopanib-Warfarin PT-INR prolongation risk, etc.) are real clinical concerns found in medical literature.
